---
title: "Building a Silicon MZM Design Flow"
description: "A system-oriented post on building a surrogate MZM flow with gdsfactory, femwell, and compact EO models."
date: 2026-03-07
categories: [silicon-photonics, mzm, simulation]
---

## Why this matters

A Mach-Zehnder modulator is not just a standalone device. It sits inside a larger link or compute system. That means the design flow should support:

- geometry and layout
- optical modes
- carrier-driven phase shift
- electrical parasitics
- compact models
- eventual system-level simulation

## Package structure

The companion Python package is `lightly_engineered`.

Useful modules include:

- `lightly_engineered.pdk.layer_stack`
- `lightly_engineered.pdk.cross_sections`
- `lightly_engineered.layout.mzm`
- `lightly_engineered.sim.optical_modes`
- `lightly_engineered.models.tline`

## Example: generating the MZM layout

```python
from lightly_engineered.layout.mzm import mzm

c = mzm()
c.show()
```

## Example: solving the optical cross section

```python
from lightly_engineered.sim.optical_modes import solve_cross_section_modes

result = solve_cross_section_modes(wavelength_um=1.31)
print(result.neff)
```

## Next steps

This post is only the beginning. The next extension is to add:

- TCAD bias sweeps
- capacitance extraction
- traveling-wave electrode models
- full-system co-design posts
